verdict: The proposal demonstrates exceptional UI specification depth. Component visual specs are implementation-ready with exact token values, dimensions, typography, and animation curves. The Phase 0 baseline addition resolves the sole prior blocker. Source code verification confirms the proposal's accuracy on ErrorBannerView raw colors, AsyncContentView degraded dead-end, and the AccessibleColors token inventory. Eight non-blocking issues remain -- all are refinement-level and addressable during implementation without proposal revision. Approve.
summary: Revision 2 resolves PO-B-01 (Phase 0 baseline recording gate) and carries forward all 30 non-blocking items plus 26 suggestions from Run 2 as implementation guidance in a new Section 11. From a UI design perspective, the proposal excels in five areas: (1) Precise visual specifications for all error components with exact token values, corner radii, padding, typography, and animation curves documented in Appendix E; (2) A comprehensive screen-by-failure-mode matrix (Section 5.2) that maps every screen to the correct error tier for each failure scenario; (3) Clear state transition animations (Section 5.3) with durations, easing curves, and haptic feedback rules; (4) A well-structured semantic font mapping table (Section 5.7) with @ScaledMetric dimensional contracts for the 77 hardcoded instances; (5) Full VoiceOver interaction specs for CoalescedErrorBannerView including tab order, dynamic announcements, and partial recovery messaging. Source code verification confirms: ErrorBannerView lines 82-98 use raw .orange/.red Color values (not AccessibleColors tokens); AsyncContentView lines 33-48 render degraded state as a dead-end with no retry/dismiss affordance; AccessibleColors.swift line 111 already defines errorBackground with adaptive light/dark opacity; OnboardingStepViews.swift has 43 hardcoded .system(size:) instances (not 40 as stated -- minor discrepancy). The phased rollout with file-overlap sequencing (5a after Phase 4, 5b parallel) and MVP cut-line for CoalescedErrorBannerView show mature risk management for a 15+ screen deployment. Eight non-blocking issues identified are all implementation-phase refinements that do not require proposal revision before work begins.

issues.title: errorBackground token already exists in AccessibleColors -- conditional language persists from Run 2
issues.description: Section 5.16 and Phase 1.10 still say 'add errorBackground token if needed' and 'add errorBackground token to AccessibleColors if it does not exist'. Source verification of AccessibleColors.swift (line 111) confirms errorBackground already exists as an adaptive status background with proper light/dark mode opacity handling (0.10 light, 0.18 dark). This was raised as UI-R2-06 in Run 2 and acknowledged in Section 11.1 but the conditional language was not fully corrected in the main specification text.
issues.suggestion: Remove all conditional 'if needed' / 'if it does not exist' language. Replace with: 'ErrorBannerView MUST use the existing AccessibleColors.errorBackground and AccessibleColors.warningBackground tokens. No new tokens required.' Implementers can resolve this trivially during Phase 1 execution.

issues.title: AsyncContentView degraded banner should explicitly require AccessibleColors tokens post-upgrade
issues.description: The current AsyncContentView degraded state (lines 37, 45) uses raw .orange foreground and .orange.opacity(0.08) background. Section 5.9 specifies upgrading the degraded state to render via ErrorBannerView internally, which would inherit the token migration from Phase 1.10. However, if the internal ErrorBannerView delegation approach changes during implementation (e.g., direct rendering for performance), the raw color usage could persist. The specification should be explicit that token compliance is required regardless of implementation approach.
issues.suggestion: Add explicit note to Section 5.9: 'Regardless of implementation approach (ErrorBannerView delegation or direct rendering), the degraded state MUST use AccessibleColors.warning foreground and AccessibleColors.warningBackground background, not raw Color values.' This is a single sentence addition.

issues.title: CoalescedErrorBannerView expanded max height of 400pt may be excessive on compact devices
issues.description: Section 5.5 specifies ScrollView capped at 400pt for expanded error list. On iPhone SE (screen height ~568pt logical) or iPhone mini (~812pt), a 400pt error panel would consume 50-70% of the screen, leaving minimal visible content below. This contradicts the design principle of keeping content visible during error states (Section 5.3: 'Content MUST remain visible'). Given the MVP fallback is likely (stacked banners, no expand/collapse), this is lower urgency but should be addressed if the full CoalescedErrorBannerView ships.
issues.suggestion: Use a proportional cap: 'maxHeight = min(400, availableHeight * 0.4)' via GeometryReader. Alternatively, specify 400pt for iPad/macOS and 250pt for iPhone. Document the platform-specific constraint.

issues.title: Success banner component treatment is scattered and underspecified
issues.description: The success banner (Section 5.3 animation table: 'Banner transitions to .success style (green, checkmark) for 1.5s, then slides up to dismiss') is referenced across multiple sections but has no dedicated visual specification. It is unclear whether this is a new ErrorCategory case on ErrorBannerView, a mode of AsyncContentView, or a standalone component. AccessibleColors has success/successBackground tokens ready, but the icon, typography, and interface are undefined. This was raised as UI-R2-08 in Run 2 and noted in Section 11.2 theme #5 but remains unspecified in the main proposal body.
issues.suggestion: Add a dedicated Section 5.21 'Success Feedback Banner Specification': (1) Extend ErrorBannerView to support a .success presentation mode (or define a lightweight SuccessBannerView); (2) Visual tokens: AccessibleColors.success foreground, AccessibleColors.successBackground background, checkmark.circle.fill icon; (3) Typography: .subheadline.semibold title only ('Updated successfully'), no message/suggestion; (4) Auto-dismiss: 1.5s default, 3s when VoiceOver active (per UX-R2-05); (5) No action buttons; (6) VoiceOver announcement before dismiss; (7) Add row to Appendix E.

issues.title: T2+T3 concurrent vertical stacking rule acknowledged but not formally incorporated
issues.description: UI-R2-03 from Run 2 recommended 'T2 banner above T3 indicator with 8pt spacing' when both ErrorBannerView and FreshnessIndicatorView are simultaneously visible. This is acknowledged in Section 11.1 non-blocking table but not formally incorporated into Section 5.13 or 5.14 where the banner positioning and freshness extension rules are defined. Four screens (DashboardView, GoalDetailView, GoalsListView, AssetDetailView) will show both indicators. Without a formal rule in the specification body, Phase 4 implementers must cross-reference Section 11 to find the guidance.
issues.suggestion: Add to Section 5.14 after 'Persistence rule': 'Stacking order: When T2 banner and T3 indicator coexist, render as VStack(spacing: 8) { ErrorBannerView; FreshnessIndicatorView }. Banner always above indicator. Banner dismiss animation slides indicator up with .spring(response: 0.3).' Two sentences, prevents per-screen inconsistency.

issues.title: Appendix E ErrorBannerView typography values incorrect vs source
issues.description: Appendix E component comparison table states ErrorBannerView typography is '.caption title, .caption2 message'. Source code verification (ErrorBannerView.swift lines 22-34) shows the actual typography is: .subheadline.semibold title (line 22-23), .caption message (line 27), .caption2 recovery suggestion (line 33). The Appendix E entry understates the title weight and misattributes the message/suggestion hierarchy. Since Appendix E serves as the implementer reference for cross-component consistency, this inaccuracy could propagate to new components that try to match ErrorBannerView.
issues.suggestion: Fix Appendix E ErrorBannerView typography to: '.subheadline.semibold title, .caption message, .caption2 suggestion'. Matches source. Implementers building CoalescedErrorBannerView and success banner will reference this table.

issues.title: 12pt font instances in onboarding lack explicit semantic mapping target
issues.description: Section 5.7 semantic font mapping table has '14pt -> .subheadline or .footnote' as the smallest entry. OnboardingStepViews.swift has three 12pt instances: difficulty badge (line 515), info labels (line 534), and StatPreview title (line 644). These have no explicit mapping target in the table. .caption renders at ~12pt at default Dynamic Type and .caption2 at ~11pt. Without a specified target, Phase 5 implementers must make ad-hoc decisions for these instances.
issues.location: Section 5.7, semantic font mapping reference table
issues.suggestion: Add row: '12pt -> .caption2 | Badge text, stat labels, tertiary info | Use .caption if 12pt is the minimum acceptable rendered size'. This covers the 3 unmapped instances explicitly.

issues.title: Post-onboarding failure empty state button layout needs adaptive spec
issues.description: Section 5.12 wireframe shows 'Try Again' and 'Create Goal Manually' side-by-side. 'Create Goal Manually' is a long label that will overflow at larger Dynamic Type sizes or on narrow devices (iPhone SE). No adaptive layout rule is specified for these buttons, unlike ErrorBannerView (Section 5.19) and the general adaptive layout guidance.
issues.suggestion: Add: 'Buttons use ViewThatFits or @Environment(\.dynamicTypeSize) to reflow from HStack to VStack at accessibility text sizes. Primary action (Try Again) appears first/top in both layouts.' Consistent with ErrorBannerView adaptive pattern.

non_blocking_issues.title: errorBackground token already exists -- remove conditional language
non_blocking_issues.recommendation: Remove 'if needed'/'if it does not exist' from Section 5.16 and Phase 1.10. State the token exists.

non_blocking_issues.title: AsyncContentView degraded banner should explicitly require AccessibleColors tokens
non_blocking_issues.recommendation: Add one sentence to Section 5.9 requiring token compliance regardless of implementation approach.

non_blocking_issues.title: CoalescedErrorBannerView 400pt max height excessive on compact devices
non_blocking_issues.recommendation: Use proportional cap (40% available height) or platform-specific values (250pt iPhone, 400pt iPad/macOS).

non_blocking_issues.title: Success banner component needs dedicated visual specification
non_blocking_issues.recommendation: Add Section 5.21 defining success banner tokens, icon, typography, auto-dismiss timing, VoiceOver behavior.

non_blocking_issues.title: T2+T3 concurrent vertical stacking rule not formally in spec body
non_blocking_issues.recommendation: Add stacking rule to Section 5.14: banner above indicator, 8pt spacing, slide-up on dismiss.

non_blocking_issues.title: Appendix E ErrorBannerView typography values incorrect vs source
12pt font instances lack explicit semantic mapping target
non_blocking_issues.recommendation: Add '12pt -> .caption2' row to Section 5.7 mapping table.

non_blocking_issues.title: Post-onboarding failure empty state buttons need adaptive layout spec
non_blocking_issues.recommendation: Add ViewThatFits HStack->VStack reflow rule consistent with ErrorBannerView Section 5.19.

suggestions.title: Define ErrorComponentTokens struct for shared layout constants in Phase 1
suggestions.description: Appendix E documents fragmented layout values across 5 error components (padding: 4-24pt, cornerRadius: 0-12pt). Extracting shared values into ErrorComponentTokens { bannerPadding: 12, bannerCornerRadius: 10, itemSpacing: 8, contentSpacing: 12 } creates a single source of truth before Phase 4 mass deployment. This was recommended as UI-R2-01/theme #6 in Run 2 and is acknowledged in Section 11.2 but worth re-emphasizing as a Phase 1 deliverable.

suggestions.title: Add dark mode verification step to Phase 7
suggestions.description: UI-R2-05 flagged missing dark mode verification. Phase 7 still has no explicit dark mode step. Add Phase 7.8b: dark mode visual verification for error/freshness components on P0 screens. AccessibleColors uses different opacity (0.10 light vs 0.18 dark) -- visual confirmation is needed.

suggestions.title: Specify ErrorBannerView dismiss button minimum touch target
suggestions.description: The dismiss button (xmark icon, .caption font, .plain button style at line 60-64) may fall below the 44x44pt touch target. Adding .frame(minWidth: 44, minHeight: 44) and .contentShape(Rectangle()) would guarantee WCAG compliance before deploying to 15+ screens.

suggestions.title: Consider ViewThatFits as primary adaptive layout approach for Section 5.19
suggestions.description: Section 5.19 offers ViewThatFits or @Environment(\.dynamicTypeSize) conditional for ErrorBannerView reflow. ViewThatFits is cleaner (no magic breakpoint, system-determined layout fit) and should be the primary recommendation. The environment approach works as fallback if ViewThatFits causes measurement issues.

suggestions.title: Specify MonthlyExecutionView unsynced badge visual details
suggestions.description: Section 5.20 mentions a 'small cloud.slash icon badge' for unsynced contributions but omits size, color, and position. Recommend: trailing position vertically centered in row, @ScaledMetric(relativeTo: .caption) 14pt icon, AccessibleColors.warning foreground, with .accessibilityLabel('Not yet synced').

suggestions.title: Align reduced-scope banner (Section 5.17) styling with FreshnessIndicatorView for visual consistency
suggestions.description: Both serve informational purposes (T3-level, non-actionable). Making FreshnessIndicatorView configurable with icon/color/text parameters would allow it to render both 'Updated X minutes ago' and 'Showing popular cryptocurrencies' variants, reducing component count. Alternatively, if kept separate, use identical .caption2 font, .tertiary foreground to create visual coherence for informational-tier indicators.

assumptions: The existing ErrorBannerView source (99 lines, verified) is the canonical version. No forks or variants exist beyond CompactChartErrorView (being deprecated).

AccessibleColors.swift is the single source of truth for design tokens. It already contains errorBackground (line 111), warningBackground (line 105), successBackground (line 99), and success (line 42) -- all with adaptive light/dark implementations.

The hardcoded .system(size:) count in onboarding is 48 (5 in OnboardingFlowView + 43 in OnboardingStepViews), not the stated 45. This minor discrepancy does not affect the scope or effort estimate materially (43 vs 40 in OnboardingStepViews is 3 additional instances).
The CoalescedErrorBannerView MVP fallback (stacked ErrorBannerViews) is the likely shipping implementation per PO-NB-01 guidance. The full collapsible component with expand/collapse is stretch scope.

The phased approach means UI consistency will be temporarily degraded between Phase 4 (new error patterns on some screens) and Phase 5 (accessibility remediation). This is acceptable given the incremental merge strategy and each PR being independently shippable.
The @ScaledMetric clamping formula inversion (UI-R2-04 from Run 2: min/max swapped in Step 5.4) is acknowledged in Section 11.1 and will be corrected during Phase 5 implementation.
